content: Autonomous agents can evolve their own long-term behavioral competence by iteratively compressing and summarizing past task and motion planning experiences the same way human memory compresses episodic details to retain core actionable insights for long-term recall. This compression process can be actively guided by the agent’s intrinsic curiosity, which prioritizes summarizing high-value unexpected experiences over redundant routine interactions.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
trigger_topic: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
